mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on future earnings and lifts equity multiples, so falling policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 1-3 month window but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive more often than baseline, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year period, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE captures deteriorating household income and weaker demand, which reduces expected corporate sales and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the relationship flips in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS rise together, higher retail sales and payroll growth indicate broad demand acceleration that supports revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-growth condition does not outperform either series alone for predicting 60-day returns, or if triggered observations fail to deliver a positive hit rate above 60% over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness signals supply-side normalization and stronger earnings leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 1.0% over the prior 2 months after a negative 3-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO rebound episodes are not followed by above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in HOUST reflects housing demand and credit-sensitive spending, which improves growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained HOUST uptrends are followed by negative or flat 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal loses explanatory power after controlling for its own past trend.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound, real rates fall and financial conditions stay loose, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.5 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-policyrate spread episodes do not produce positive excess 20-day returns versus baseline, or if the interaction term is not statistically stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A combined rise in PAYEMS and a fall in UNRATE confirms labor-market improvement, which raises expected consumption and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if joint employment-improvement episodes fail to outperform single-series signals at the 5-day horizon, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 signals broad output contraction and weaker future earnings, so negative GDP surprises predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDP-growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign is not stable across recessionary subperiods.
